history.qwen_observation: The build failed at the first step because maven-compiler-plugin:3.7.0 does not support the --release flag, and executing under JDK 8 causes javac to reject it.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading the compiler plugin to 3.11.0 and switching the first step's execution JDK to 11 resolves the --release incompatibility, allowing the Lombok version bump to apply and compile successfully before proceeding with the Java 11 migration.

history.qwen_observation: All 41 tests failed post-migration despite successful compilation, indicating missing framework upgrades (Spring Boot and Jakarta EE namespace) required for Java 21 compatibility.
history.qwen_rationale: The original chain skipped Spring Boot and Jakarta migrations, causing test context initialization failures. Inserting a progressive Spring Boot upgrade chain (2.7 -> 3.0 -> 3.2) alongside the Java version bumps will handle the javax-to-jakarta migration and update framework configurations, resolving the test failures.

history.qwen_observation: Step `spring_boot_to_2_7` failed because Spring Boot 2.7 removes `RepositoryRestConfigurerAdapter` and HATEOAS `Resource`/`Resources`, which the codebase still references, and the upgrade recipe did not auto-migrate these deprecated APIs.
history.qwen_rationale: Skipping the problematic 2.7 intermediate step and jumping directly to Spring Boot 3.0 on JDK 17 leverages the more comprehensive code migrations bundled in the 3.0 recipe (including HATEOAS/Data REST modernization and javax->jakarta namespace shifts). Bumping the build configuration to Java 17 first ensures the compiler and dependencies align with SB 3.0's hard requirement, avoiding the adapter removal breakage while maintaining a safe trajectory to 3.2 and Java 21.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at spring_boot_to_3_0 due to an unresolved and incompatible explicit dependency on hibernate-core:5.3.1.Final.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.0 requires Hibernate 6.x and manages it via its BOM. Removing the explicit hibernate-core dependency allows Spring Boot's dependency management to inject a compatible version, resolving the dependency resolution error and aligning with SB3 requirements.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at spring_boot_to_3_0 because UpgradeSpringBoot_3_0 changed the Hibernate groupId to org.hibernate.orm but retained the incompatible version 5.3.1.Final, causing a dependency resolution failure.
history.qwen_rationale: Changing the RemoveDependency step to target the original Hibernate 5 groupId (org.hibernate) will eliminate the conflicting explicit dependency before the Spring Boot 3.0 migration, allowing the Spring Boot BOM to manage the correct Hibernate 6 version automatically.
